title: How to make a first date exciting, based on psychology
text: welcome back to my channel I'm Anna I'm a clinical psychology doctorate student and I make videos about real-world applications to psychology so in this video I'm gonna be talking about the one thing you should do on a first date based on a psychological study investigating theories of emotion so in 1974 Dutton and Aaron did a study in which they had an attractive woman approach men either on a bridge a very shaky long unsteady bridge or towards the end and she did what's called a TT card so she gave them an image and asked them to make a story about it and then she also gave them her phone number rest up as you know for the study so what the researchers found was that the men who were still on the bridge still you know experiencing the shaky nervous adrenaline rush of the bridge tended to give much more sexual responses to the t-80 card and they tended to call the woman more so what was the reason for this it's actually pretty simple they were physiologically aroused meaning they had rapid heart rate you know sweating adrenaline all that good stuff because of the bridge and how I was making them nervous I mean you know the sense of danger and adrenaline and they miss attributed this physiological arousal as attraction for the woman this is actually called now misattribution of arousal because of the study so what was the point of the study anyway it was to look at theories of emotion so psychologists have different theories about how we come to experience an emotion so let me give some examples you see a bear you feel your heart racing and you attribute this to fear that's how one psychologist may see it but another psychologist might see it as you see a bear you feel fear and your heart starts to race because of that fear or it could also be perceived as you see a bear and your heart starts to race you feel fear at the same time so in this case the theory is that you experience this event you experience physiological arousal so racing heart adrenalin sweating you were interpret this as being due to the attractive female and not a shaky bridge and then you label this emotion as attraction so what does this mean for your dating life well it's pretty simple I actually don't have all that much to say

title: How to make a first date exciting, based on psychology
text: ou were interpret this as being due to the attractive female and not a shaky bridge and then you label this emotion as attraction so what does this mean for your dating life well it's pretty simple I actually don't have all that much to say on it the one thing you should do on a first date or if you want to get someone basically pricked into being attracted to you is do something that gets them physiologically aroused so if you're gonna go on a date still just go to dinner and a movie go to a roller coaster or a water park or go on a hike this can not only make you bond over something simpler than just being nervous over dinner and asking so tell me about your family but it also gets their body excited which gets them thinking oh maybe I'm excited about this person actually obviously you don't take this to mean that if you take someone somewhere where they're gonna get their heart racing you're they're automatically going to fall for you or be attracted to you that's not the case it's just as a trend it tends to be that when we do things that get ass physiologically aroused there is a chance that we might miss attribute that arousal for attraction to the person we're with and obviously you're not going to be only interacting with this person in scenarios where you're physiologically aroused so keep that in mind because if you ever do have to just stay a night in and watch Netflix and have dinner that might be an issue but anyway that's just all I had to say today please hit like and subscribe if you like the same of content I will see you next time
